title: Request Construction, Method Semantics, Safety, and Idempotency · Go Client Systems · Language Lens: Preconditions Concurrency
summary: A deployment controller creates and cancels rollout jobs, where duplicate writes could launch the same change twice. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go preconditions concurrency increment in a Go 1.26 net/http method-aware request builder. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply If-Match, If-None-Match, and related preconditions to prevent lost updates and duplicate effects. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Choose POST, PUT, PATCH, or DELETE from resource, replacement, partial-change, and repeat behavior. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Preconditions Concurrency · Theory Preconditions Concurrency
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model must repeat request G-2 after an uncertain timeout: GET status, POST job creation, PUT replacement, and PATCH transition are candidates. Classify safety, idempotency, body replay, and If-Match evidence before choosing.

content.text: State method semantics, target authority, credential scope, body ownership, byte and time budgets, and expected status or schema; trace resolution, transport, redirects, and attempts; then test one changed failure with deterministic cleanup evidence. Apply that move specifically to Preconditions Concurrency while producing a Go 1.26 net/http method-aware request builder.
